name: Knot
description: Interactive coaching course for knot compiled in real-time from webcam footage.

steps.name: Forming the Initial Loop
steps.description: Select a suitable length of cord or string, hold both ends in your hands, and cross them over to form a clear, open circular loop in the center.
steps.voice_guideline: Step 1: Forming the Loop. Select a length of cord, hold both ends, and cross them over to form a clear circular loop.
steps.illustrative_image_prompt: Vector drawing of a simple open loop formed with a green rope, crossed at the bottom.

checklist.title: Circular Loop Formed
checklist.description: Verify that the cord forms a complete, stable circular loop with the two ends crossing over each other.

failure_conditions.trigger_description: The loop is too small or the ends are uneven, making it difficult to thread the knot in subsequent steps.
failure_conditions.mitigation_instruction: Ensure you leave at least 10-15 centimeters of working cord on each end and keep the central loop wide and loose.
